response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable full-stack engineer with genuine breadth across languages and platforms, but falls well short of the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) bar. The role demands director-level strategic leadership, recognized AI expertise with demonstrated shipped products, and deep cloud platform strategy experience. Her AI exposure is limited to tooling contributions that began only in April 2025, her career arc is consistently that of a senior/staff individual contributor rather than a strategic technical leader, and there is no evidence of advising strategic customers or setting organizational technical direction at the required scale.
strengths: Genuine polyglot engineer with consistent delivery across web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance systems spanning roughly 16 years
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and sustained GISCollective as a real open-source geo-data platform serving NGO users, with a public conference talk at FOSS4G 2019
Active knowledge-sharer with delivered workshops and public talks, demonstrating communication skills relevant to the 'inspire others' responsibility
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent — MCP tooling contributions starting April 2025 do not constitute the 'recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products or platforms' the role explicitly requires

No director-level or strategic leadership experience; career trajectory is senior/staff IC with no evidence of advising strategic customers, owning technical roadmaps at organizational scale, or cloud platform strategy
No graduate degree in an AI-related field, and cloud experience is peripheral (AWS CDK usage, Kubernetes deployments) rather than the deep cloud platform expertise the role centers on
